Embedding model not in model-profiles.json at all. Lives at G:\llamacpp (not G:\llama.cpp). Different path entirely.

environment_variables.description: Environment variables that control runtime startup and behavior

items.consumer_file_or_component: Rust router, Python router, operations scripts (indirectly via port hardcoding)
items.notes: Set by launcher. Rust router also reads as default. Operations scripts hardcode this value independently.
Defined in Rust code, overrideable via env

Boolean toggle. Tests set this to true to verify auth enforcement.
Optional override. Set by test-runtime-limits.ps1.

absolute_paths.description: All absolute Windows paths found in tracked startup files, classified by casing drift

items.notes: CASE DRIFT: G:\OpenWork (camelCase) vs launcher's G:\openwork (lowercase). Windows is case-insensitive, but this is a maintenance risk.
CASE DRIFT: G:\OpenWork (camelCase) — mismatched from launcher
Git-tracked file with machine-specific absolute path. Both casing variants present in this file.
GGUF files stored at G:\llama.cpp\models — outside the repo entirely. Git-tracked profile files contain these paths.
Different binary (llama-server-mini.exe not llama-server.exe) from a different build directory
Path uses G:\llamacpp (no . in path) — different from G:\llama.cpp used everywhere else. Different namespace entirely.
Uses G:\temp — a system temp location, not the repo
References a completely different repo (TheLibrarian-main) outside the project boundary
Gitignored local config. References 'G:\openwork\thelibrarian' which is neither 'OpenWork' nor 'openwork' — a third casing variant.
An example should use relative paths or placeholder variables, not absolute machine paths

path_casing_drift.description: Summary of all path casing variations found across tracked files
path_casing_drift.recommendation: Normalize to a single casing (G:\OpenWork) or remove absolute paths entirely
